name: Hardener
description: Cross-platform security auditing and hardening tool written in Go. Load a ruleset, run an audit, apply fixes, and roll back if needed — all from a single binary with no external dependencies. Supports Ubuntu, Debian, Rocky Linux, ArchLinux, RHEL, Fedora, and macOS. Published as ERNW Whitepaper #77.

name: Cherry Picker
description: Smart IP selection tool for penetration testers built during the CERN Whitehat Challenge. Leverages entropy filtering, TTL clustering, and cloud targeting for web enumeration & OSINT. Still in early development — the idea is solid, the time is scarce.

name: Forensic Log Tracker
description: Modular, secure CLI tool for forensic professionals and students. Implements cryptographic GPG signing and hash verification for legally consistent documentation. Used in university-level forensic training at Hochschule Mannheim.

name: ERNW Whitepaper #77 — Unified Security Hardening with Cross-Platform Native Binaries
description: Introduces the Hardener tool and the Documentation-as-Code approach: hardening logic embedded in Markdown, distributed as a static binary with atomic rollback. Validated across Ubuntu, Debian, Rocky Linux, openSUSE, Arch Linux, Fedora, and RHEL.

name: ERNW Whitepaper #76 — Linux Client Hardening Guide
description: Covers six security domains — authentication, network security, boot integrity, OS hardening, filesystem permissions, and application security — with mandatory and recommended controls per RFC 2119. Prioritises practical hardening over theoretical compliance.

name: ERNW Whitepaper #75 — macOS Tahoe Hardening Guide
description: Co-authored with Julian Suleder. Comprehensive technical guide balancing usability with security, detailing configurations to minimise the macOS attack surface on Apple systems.
